title: Robust Shaggy Canadian Onion Poutine
description: A hearty, mustardy Canadian dish featuring caramelized onions, crispy fries, and dairy-free cheese curds, topped with a bonito shave for added depth of flavor. This show-stopping poutine is sure to impress.

ingredients.name: french fries
ingredients.notes: thick-cut, preferably from a high-starch potato variety

ingredients.name: dairy-free cheese curds
ingredients.notes: made from tapioca starch and coconut oil

ingredients.name: bonito flakes
ingredients.notes: shaved over the dish just before serving

ingredients.name: olive oil
ingredients.notes: for frying the onions and fries

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Toss the french fries with a tablespoon of olive oil and season with salt, then spread out in a single layer on a baking sheet and bake for 20 minutes, or until crispy.
While the fries are baking, heat the remaining olive oil in a large skillet over medium-low heat and caramelize the onions, stirring occasionally, for 30 minutes, or until deep golden brown.
In a small bowl, whisk together the mustard and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Once the fries are done, remove them from the oven and increase the oven temperature to broil.
In a large bowl, toss the fries with the caramelized onions, dairy-free cheese curds, and a drizzle of the mustard mixture.
Transfer the poutine mixture to a broiler-safe dish and broil for 2-3 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the poutine from the oven and top with shaved bonito flakes.
Serve immediately, garnished with chopped scallions if desired.
